phrasal_verbs.meaning: make a copy of information on your computer; give support to someone
move things so that they are in different places or positions
stop being in one state and start being in another; put on different clothes
take off clothes and put on different ones
repair, paint and improve an old building; fasten clothing
put information into a computer using keys
put things together without order; think one person/thing is another
test something to see if it works correctly
change or develop into something different
use something a lot so it no longer works

idioms.idiom: a leopard can't change its spots
idioms.meaning: someone will never change their behaviour or character
machines in your house that make life easy
do something very different from what is usually done
waste time trying to do something already done well
refuse to change what you are saying or doing
skills and equipment needed to do your job
change your life by starting to be a better person

M.title: Word formation (text)
M.instructions: Complete the text by writing the correct form of each word in capitals.
M.passage: We often think of ourselves as living in a time of (1) ______ (CONTINUE) technological change. We tend to believe that we are unique in history in dealing with a constantly (2) ______ (EVOLVE) world of gadgets. However, the end of the nineteenth century was also a time that saw many (3) ______ (REVOLT) changes. People had needed to show (4) ______ (FLEXIBLE) throughout the nineteenth century, as the effects of the Industrial Revolution meant constantly making (5) ______ (ADJUST) to deal with changing working conditions. Towards the end of the century, though, people had to become more (6) ______ (ADAPT) than ever before. The typewriter, the telephone, the electric light bulb and other (7) ______ (INFLUENCE) developments gave people the (8) ______ (CAPABLE) to live and work in ways their grandparents could not have imagined. Over the next 30 years, little remained (9) ______ (ALTER) as the camera, the cinema, the phonograph, the plane and radio all had an (10) ______ (ELECTRIC) effect on people and society.

questions.sentence: 1 (CONTINUE)
2 (EVOLVE)
3 (REVOLT)
4 (FLEXIBLE)
5 (ADJUST)
6 (ADAPT)
7 (INFLUENCE)
8 (CAPABLE)
9 (ALTER)
10 (ELECTRIC)

N.title: Word formation
N.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals.

questions.sentence: The weather's been so ______ today that I don't know what to wear. (CHANGE)
Bonnie showed a lot of ______ in the way she handled the problem with Olivia. (MATURE)
The flats were built in the 1960s, but they were all ______ in the 90s. (MODERN)
Jack got in a lot of trouble for ______ breaking school rules. (PERSIST)
I really don't think I'd have the ______ to finish a marathon! (ENDURE)
I feel a lot better since I decided to stop eating ______ food. (PROCESS)
Can we ______ the lesson for next Wednesday? (ARRANGE)
We need to rely more on wave power, wind power and other ______ sources of energy. (NEW)
I gradually lost touch with Ricardo after his ______ to Islam. (CONVERT)
I'm scanning my gran's photos onto my computer because they'd be ______ if anything happened to them. (PLACE)
